studyName: Coagulation and Fibrinolysis in a Pediatric Insulin Titration Trial

tables.description: This subject phenotypes data table reports each subject's HALF_PINT treatment group, race, AGE at ICU admission, primary reason for ICU admission, height at ICU admission, actual weight at ICU admission, type of invasive mechanical ventilation at randomization, primary reason for initiation of mechanical ventilation,  oxygenation index (OI) on the day of initiation of mechanical ventilation, oxygen saturation index (OSI) on the day of initiation of mechanical ventilation, Pediatric Acute Respiratory Distress Syndrome (PARDS) based on OI or OSI on the day of initiation of mechanical ventilation, tracheostomy status for overnight/sleep prior to illness that led to ICU admission, status of ionotropic support for hypotension at randomization, primary reason for hypotension, pediatric risk of mortality (PRISM) III-12 score, risk of death in the ICU based on PRISM III-12 score, status for positive culture drawn within 72 hrs preceeding randomization and 24 hrs post, site of positive culture, organism of positive culture, location of hospital discharge assessed at day 90, viability status on or before day 90, average daily insulin doasage, time weighted glucose average (mg/dL), ICU-free days through day 28, ICU days through day 28, ventilator-free days through day 28, ventilator days through day 28, hospital-free days through day 28, hospital days through day 28, hospital mortality at day 28, maximum pediatric logistic organ dysfunction (PELOD) score, maximum daily vasoactive ionotrope score, ICU length of stay for survivors, multiple organ dysfunction (MODS) group, and status of sepsis.

variables.name: ageyrs
variables.description: Age at ICU admission in years

variables.name: death28
variables.description: Whether or not the patient was deceased on or before day 28 (Hospital mortality at day 28)

variables.name: death90
variables.description: Whether or not the patient was deceased on or before day 90 (Hospital mortality at day 90)

variables.name: hospdisloc
variables.description: Location of hospital discharge (assessed at day 90)

variables.name: hypotcons
variables.description: Whether or not the patient received ionotropic support for hypotension at randomization (Recoded for 7 patients 8/12/20)

variables.name: iculos
variables.description: ICU length of stay for survivors (from randomization to date/time of physical ICU discharge)

variables.name: intubvent
variables.description: Type of invasive mechanical ventilation at randomization

variables.name: maxpelod
variables.description: Maximum Pediatric Logistic Organ Dysfunction (PELOD) score

variables.name: noninv
variables.description: Whether or not the patient was on non-invasive ventilation that provided at least 5 cm H2O prior to illness that led to ICU admission

variables.name: oifm03
variables.description: Oxygenation index (OI) on the day of initiation of mechanical ventilation (if mechanically ventilated)

variables.name: osifm03
variables.description: Oxygen saturation index (OSI) on the day of initiation of mechanical ventilation (if mechanically ventilated)

variables.name: pardsfm03
variables.description: Pediatric Acute Respiratory Distress Syndrome (PARDS) based on OI or OSI on the day of initiation of mechanical ventilation (if mechanically ventilated)

variables.name: poscult
variables.description: Whether or not a positive culture was drawn within 72 hours preceding randomization and 24 hours post randomization

variables.name: primaryhypot
variables.description: Primary reason for hypotension, If receiving ionotropic support (Recoded for 4 patients 8/12/20)

variables.name: primarymv
variables.description: Primary reason for initiation of mechanical ventilation (if mechanically ventilated)

variables.name: prismrom
variables.description: Risk of death in the ICU according to PRISM III-12 score (%)

variables.name: prismscore
variables.description: Pediatric Risk of Mortality (PRISM) III-12 score

variables.name: ventnight
variables.description: Whether or not the patient was supported with ventilation via tracheostomy overnight/during sleep prior to illness that led to ICU admission

variables.name: wt
variables.description: Actual (not dosing) weight at ICU admission (kg)

tables.description: This sample attributes data table reports each sample's body site of origin, analyte type, tumor status, and subject age at collection.

variables.name: BODY_SITE
variables.description: Body site where sample was collected

tables.description: This subject consent data table contains subject IDs, consent information, and biological sex.

variables.name: CONSENT
variables.description: Consent group as determined by DAC

tables.description: This subject sample mapping data table includes a mapping of study subject IDs to sample IDs. Samples are the final preps submitted for genotyping, sequencing, and/or expression data. For example, if one patient (subject ID) gave one sample, and that sample was processed differently to generate 2 sequencing runs, there would be two rows, both using the same subject ID, but having 2 unique sample IDs.
